attacking_style: Pure sprinter who does not initiate attacks; relies entirely on positioning in final kilometers to contest bunch sprints when gradients permit.
team_tactics: Team commits resources to lead-out trains in sprint opportunities, with Mitchelton-Scott historically organizing full sprint trains for him. Positioning work evident but execution often falls short.
terrain_preference: Flat bunch sprint finishes where positioning matters more than raw power. Exposed on any gradient or technical finale requiring explosive acceleration.
vulnerability: Finishing speed significantly below top-tier sprinters even when perfectly positioned. Crash-prone in hectic sprint positioning phases, losing team investment.
key_patterns: Achieves strong positioning in sprint setups but lacks final 200m speed to convert (2 instances)
Team invests lead-out resources but results fail to match organizational effort (2 instances)
Capable of making front splits in harder races but cannot finish competitively (2 instances)
form_trajectory: negative: 2020 showed improved positioning reaching front splits, but finishing speed deficit widened significantly in sprints
